description:
You are working as a platform-engineer who is tightening the security checks that run in the CI/CD
    pipeline.  
    A small demo project already lives in the filesystem:

      /home/user/project/README.md
      /home/user/project/.env                (contains a deliberately hard-coded secret)
      /home/user/project/app.py

    Goal  
    ----  
    1. Search the file /home/user/project/.env for the presence of an **AWS secret access key**.  
       A valid key is any line that contains the literal text “AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY”.  
    2. Store the scan result in a new directory  
          /home/user/security_logs/  
       under the file name  
          /home/user/security_logs/secret_scan.log
    3. Use no more than **three** shell commands in total (the
       exact commands are up to you).

    Required output format (the test suite is strict)  
    -----------------------------------------------  
    • If at least one secret is found you must write **exactly one** block per secret, with the block formatted like this (no extra spaces!):

        Filename: <absolute path to the file>
        Line: <line number where the secret was found>
        SecretType: AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY

      Example (illustrative):

        Filename: /home/user/project/.env
        Line: 3
        SecretType: AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY

    • If no secret is found the file must contain this single line (all caps):

        NO_SECRETS_FOUND

    Notes & constraints  
    -------------------  
    • Create the directory /home/user/security_logs/ if it does not already exist.  
    • Overwrite /home/user/security_logs/secret_scan.log if it is already present.  
    • Do not add any extra lines, headers or blank lines to the log file.  
    • Remember: only three commands maximum may be executed.  
    • You do NOT need elevated privileges; everything happens under /home/user.  
    • Once finished, you can open the log file to display its contents so a human can visually confirm the result, but that is optional.
truth:
Initial file tree before the agent starts:

      /home/user/project/
        ├── README.md
        ├── .env
        └── app.py

    Contents of /home/user/project/.env (three lines, \n line endings):

        # Demo environment file
        DB_PASSWORD=supersecret
        AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=ABCD1234SECRETTEST


    The automated checker will verify:

    1. A directory /home/user/security_logs/ exists (mode 755 or more open).
    2. A file /home/user/security_logs/secret_scan.log exists and is a regular file.
    3. The file’s content is **exactly** (no trailing spaces):

        Filename: /home/user/project/.env
        Line: 3
        SecretType: AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY

       with a final newline (Unix LF) after the last line.
name: task_000000_2fbbc547
